description: Standard onboarding — HR Generalist in Holland
request: Onboard Danniell Mayhew as an HR Generalist in Holland, starting 2026-06-15, manager is slopez
criteria: create_ad_user must be called before assign_ad_groups
assign_ad_groups and provision_exchange_mailbox must both complete before assign_m365_license
User is created with email danniell.mayhew@mockcompany.com and OU=Users,OU=Holland
M365 license assigned is Microsoft365_E3 per MOCKCO HR policy
Final response includes Summary, Actions Taken, Outstanding Items, and Credentials sections
notes: Confirmed in live run 2026-06-11. Agent correctly parallelized independent tool calls on turns 1 and 3 (get_onboarding_template + get_department_policies, then assign_ad_groups + provision_exchange_mailbox). License correctly assigned as E3 per MOCKCO HR policy.

description: IT Admin onboarding — dual account creation
request: Onboard a new IT Admin named Jamie Torres in Grand Rapids
criteria: create_ad_user automatically creates both jtorres and jtorres-admin
jtorres-admin is placed in OU=Admins and receives no mailbox or M365 license
jtorres receives Microsoft365_E5 per MOCKCO IT policy
Groups assigned include SG-IT-Full and SG-Role-ITAdmin
notes: create_ad_user auto-created jtorres-admin in OU=Admins as expected. E5 license correctly assigned to the standard account only; no mailbox/license provisioning was attempted for the admin account.

request: Immediately offboard rwilson — security incident, suspected account compromise
criteria: revoke_access is called BEFORE disable_ad_user (emergency protocol)
revoke_access is called with emergency=true and offboard_type="security_incident"
revoke_access result includes INCIDENT_RESPONSE_TRIGGERED action
Final response notes priority=IMMEDIATE
notes: Agent correctly detected emergency keywords ("immediately", "security incident", "compromise") and reversed the standard order — revoke before disable. INCIDENT_RESPONSE_TRIGGERED action present in revoke_access result.

request: Offboard tpatel — voluntary resignation, last day June 20, delegate to cthompson
criteria: disable_ad_user is called BEFORE revoke_access (standard protocol — opposite of TC-03)
revoke_access is called with offboard_type="resignation"
Mailbox is converted to shared with forwarding/delegation noted for cthompson
OneDrive transfer to cthompson is noted as an action or outstanding item
notes: Order is the opposite of TC-03 as expected for a standard resignation. Mailbox conversion to shared and OneDrive transfer to cthompson were both reflected in the revoke_access result and the final summary.

description: Status check — existing user by full name
request: What is the current status of Sandra Lopez?
criteria: Only one tool call is made
Partial name match resolves "Sandra Lopez" to username slopez
Response includes location, groups, license, and last login
notes: Single tool call, no unnecessary lookups. Partial name match resolved correctly to slopez. Response included all requested fields.

request: Onboard a new employee in Engineering
criteria: Agent does not call create_ad_user without a location
Agent asks for the employee's name and work location before proceeding
Agent explains why location is required (location-based OU placement at MOCKCO)
notes: Agent asked for name and location before calling any tools, citing that OU placement at MOCKCO is location-based. No premature account creation occurred.

criteria: get_user_status is called and returns found=false
Agent handles the not-found result gracefully without crashing
Agent suggests verifying the username or trying a different search term
notes: Tool returned found=false as expected. Agent reported the user could not be located and suggested double-checking the username — no crash or unhandled exception.

description: OU placement bug (documented failure, now fixed)
request: Onboard a new HR Coordinator in Holland
criteria: create_ad_user places the new user in OU=Users,OU=Holland (not OU=General)
Department lookup for "HR" resolves correctly regardless of casing
notes: HISTORICAL FAILURE — fixed in Session 2. In tools.py v1, department.title() converted "HR" to "Hr", causing the OU map lookup to fail and placing the user in OU=General instead of OU=Users,OU=Holland. Run also stalled before assign_m365_license due to the incorrect OU breaking a downstream check. Fixed by trying an exact-match lookup before falling back to .title() normalization. This test now passes against tools.py v2 (see TC-01 and TC-02 for confirmed passing runs against the fixed code).
